codeReview.summary: The fixed health dashboard now satisfies the milestone frontend behavior: the banner combines freshness, gaps, consistency, and calendar health; the per-stock section clearly summarizes the tracked universe while providing searchable drill-down rows for the highest-gap symbols; and the rest of the added sections match the requested asynchronous/error-isolated behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend worker skill says to use `apiFetch()` for health API calls, but the implemented dashboard uses raw `fetch()` for all new requests and the handoff still marked procedure adherence as true. Either the skill should explicitly allow same-origin `fetch` for frontend-only pages or reviewers should treat this as a real procedure deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/components/HealthDashboard.tsx uses `fetch("/api/health/...")` in `fetchData`, `fetchGaps`, `fetchFailures`, and `fetchConsistency`; /Users/wendy/work/trading-co/ashare/.factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md step 4 recommends `apiFetch('/api/health/gaps')`; fix handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance says not to restart running services, but the original frontend worker had to restart backend behavior to load already-committed health endpoints and called out the conflict. Shared instructions should clarify whether restarting an existing local dev service is allowed when necessary to validate code already in the repo.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/wendy/.factory/missions/5ccfbb11-945c-4b92-969a-cfbdf3f9d668/AGENTS.md says 'Do NOT restart running services'; original handoff for health-dashboard-frontend-upgrade lists a backend restart as part of verification and suggests clarifying the rule.

summary: Re-review passed. I reviewed the original failed scrutiny report, both worker handoffs, the fix commit diff, the current HealthDashboard frontend, the updated health gaps backend response, and the relevant shared-state docs. The prior blockers are addressed by the fix feature: banner logic now includes calendar health alongside gaps and consistency, and the per-stock area now exposes tracked/healthy/gapped counts instead of pretending the top-50 gap list is the whole universe. No new blocking issues were found in the reviewed feature scope.
